html:
Muhammad ordered all dogs killed, then reversed for hunting and farm dogs Strange / Obscure Contradiction Moderate Abu Dawud #2845
"The Messenger ordered all the dogs in Medina be killed. He then granted permission for hunting dogs..."

What the hadith says

Muhammad initially commanded the mass killing of all dogs, then revised the order to permit hunting, farm, and shepherd dogs while maintaining a prohibition on pet dogs.

Why this is a problem

An absolute prophetic command — kill all dogs — was reconsidered and partially reversed. Mass animal culling based on religious command is extreme; its subsequent softening reveals that the command was iterative policy rather than timeless divine ordinance. The result is a patchwork: some dogs are licit, others must be killed. Classical jurisprudence inherited the patchwork and has never rationalized it into a coherent principle.

Why it fails

A public-health measure does not require a categorical divine command then a divine exception — it requires a regulation. The framing as prophetic command followed by prophetic reversal means either the first command was wrong (prophets err) or the second was a revision of divine will (revelation is revisable). Neither supports the claimed infallibility of the prophetic instruction.

Abu Dawud confirms the fly-wing cure: one wing disease, the other the antidote Science Claims Strange / Obscure Moderate Abu Dawud #3844
"One wing has disease, the other has cure. Dip the fly fully."

What the hadith says

If a fly lands in a drink, one wing carries disease and the other the cure — dipping the fly fully neutralizes the contamination. Abu Dawud preserves this parallel to the Bukhari version.

Why this is a problem

Flies carry pathogens on their entire body, including legs and body hair, not differentially on one wing. Dunking the fly more thoroughly increases contamination; it does not neutralize it. This advice, followed literally, is a recipe for infection. The hadith survives at sahih grade in multiple collections, meaning it cannot be dismissed as weak material — it is the tradition's most authenticated category of claim, and it is wrong.

Why it fails

The studies cited are preliminary and contested; no mainstream microbiology supports the claim that fly-dunking neutralizes contamination. The "Muhammad anticipated science" defense is the predictable response when prophetic medical claims fail — and it shifts the burden of proof. A false claim preserved at the highest hadith grade is not redeemed by speculative future science; it remains a false claim at the highest hadith grade.

Trees and rocks betray hiding Jews to Muslims for slaughter at end-times Strange / Obscure Treatment of Disbelievers Moderate Abu Dawud end-times corpus
"The tree and the rock will say: 'O Muslim — there is a Jew behind me. Come and kill him.'"

What the hadith says

At the end of time, trees and rocks will speak to identify Jews hiding behind them, calling on Muslims to kill them. The Gharkad tree alone will remain silent, because it is the tree of the Jews.

Why this is a problem

The hadith is cross-preserved in Bukhari, Muslim, and Abu Dawud. Article 7 of the 1988 Hamas Charter quotes it verbatim as ideological justification. Trees and inanimate objects are portrayed as complicit agents in the ethnic killing of Jews at the end of history. This is not metaphor: classical commentary treats it as eschatological reality. The tradition cannot prevent this use because the hadith is accurately preserved and accurately quoted.

Why it fails

Hamas did not add the ethnic specificity — it is in the text. The hadith names Jews (al-yahud), not an abstract adversary, and directs their killing. Eschatological framing does not neutralize a command to kill an identified group. A canonical text that names a specific people for slaughter and has been used verbatim in a founding charter of a political-military organization has done ideological work; the theological context does not undo it.

When the sun rises from the west, repentance is no longer accepted Jesus / Christology Contradiction Moderate Abu Dawud #4310
"When the sun rises from the west, no repentance will be accepted."

What the hadith says

A major sign of the Hour: the sun rises from the west, after which Allah closes the door of repentance permanently.

Why this is a problem

The sun rising from the west requires the Earth's rotation to reverse — a cataclysm that would end all life before any theological consequence could follow. The sign is physically impossible without first destroying the civilized world that would witness the door of mercy closing. The hadith also contradicts Islamic teaching on divine mercy: a God who closes repentance at a cosmological trigger has built an arbitrary deadline into a supposedly infinite mercy.

Why it fails

"Divine miracle" is the universal rescue for physically impossible hadith claims. When every physically false prediction can be reframed as miracle, the predictions become unfalsifiable by construction. A prophecy whose fulfillment requires physically impossible conditions that are then excused as miracles is a prophecy that has retreated from any claim about the actual world.

Stoning rests on a claimed-missing Quranic verse no longer in the text Women Violence Contradiction Moderate Abu Dawud #4415
"We used to recite a verse about stoning. But we cannot find it in the Quran."

What the hadith says

The Quran prescribes 100 lashes for adultery (24:2). The stoning penalty derives from a verse companions say they once recited but can no longer find in the text — a claimed "removed verse" whose ruling remained even as its text disappeared.

Why this is a problem

The death penalty for adultery rests on a verse companions admit is absent from the current Quran. The doctrine of naskh al-tilawa (recitation abrogated, ruling retained) was invented to explain this gap — but it undermines the Quran's self-description as complete and preserved (15:9). Modern Islamic law implements the harsher stoning penalty over the Quran's explicit lashing prescription, on the authority of a text nobody can produce.

Why it fails

A legal system whose most severe criminal penalty rests on a text that is not in the book — preserved only in the memory of companions who say they used to read it — has conceded that the Quran is incomplete. The multiple-companion attestation is not a Quranic verse; it is hadith evidence for a verse that is not there. Executing people on the authority of an absent text is not preserving revelation; it is substituting memory for scripture.

Night raid permission: women and children share combatant status Violence Prophetic Character Moderate Abu Dawud #2672
"[Asked about] attacking pagan settlements at night — children and women killed... The Prophet said: 'They are from them.'"

What the hadith says

Asked specifically about night raids where women and children would be killed, Muhammad replied that civilian family members share the combatant status of their community — "they are from them."

Why this is a problem

The civilian-combatant distinction is the foundational principle of humanitarian law. This hadith collapses that distinction by group membership: belonging to the enemy community transfers legal combatant status to non-fighters. Night raids are inherently indiscriminate; this permission covers the worst-case scenario. Modern extremist groups have cited this hadith to justify civilian casualties — they are citing it accurately.

Why it fails

The questioner asked about raids where women and children "are killed" — Muhammad said "they are from them." That is not a permission for incidental deaths; it is a statement removing their protected status. The distinction between incidental and deliberate killing collapses precisely in night raids, where the questioner noted distinguishing combatants from families is impossible. The hadith is in the corpus, accurately preserved, and continues to provide textual warrant for civilian targeting.

Muhammad supervised the beheading of 600–900 Banu Qurayza men Prophetic Character Violence Moderate Abu Dawud Banu Qurayza corpus
"The Prophet watched the execution of each of them at the trenches he had dug."

What the hadith says

After the siege of the Banu Qurayza, Muhammad supervised the mass execution of between 600 and 900 men, who were beheaded individually in trenches dug for the purpose.

Why this is a problem

The tradition preserves this episode without presenting it as morally troubling. Muhammad not only authorized the execution but personally oversaw it. By modern standards — and by the standards of most 7th-century traditions that would find mass supervised execution extreme — this is a defining act of the prophet's character. Apologetics typically reach for "they violated the treaty" as justification; the tradition itself records the prophet watching each beheading, not a reluctant authorization from a distance.

Why it fails

The tradition records Muhammad accepting and watching the executions — the moral responsibility does not pass to Sa'd by delegation. A prophet who personally observes 600–900 executions one by one is exercising active oversight, not passive acceptance. Treaty violation may justify military response; it does not require personal supervision of mass beheadings.

Asma bint Marwan — a nursing mother assassinated for poetry against Muhammad Prophetic Character Violence Women Moderate Abu Dawud sirah parallels
[From early Islamic biography:] "The assassin came at night while her infant was still at her breast; he stabbed her, removing the infant first."

What the hadith says

Asma bint Marwan, a mother of five who composed satirical verses against Muhammad, was assassinated at night while nursing her infant. Muhammad's reported response was: "Two goats will not butt heads over her" — indifference to her death.

Why this is a problem

The victim was a nursing mother targeted for poetic criticism. The assassination combined the categories most protected in Islam's own norms: a woman, a nursing mother, killed for words rather than arms. Muhammad's dismissive response is preserved as approval. The tradition records the episode not as a moral failure but as a justified act against a satirist. The precedent it sets for literary dissent and for the protected status of women is clear.

Why it fails

The event is preserved in enough early Islamic biographical sources that total dismissal requires motivated skepticism. "Active incitement" as a category for satirical verse is itself the problem being assessed: a tradition that treats poetry against its prophet as incitement warranting assassination has already answered the question about its relationship to criticism.

Muhammad could not pray for his own mother — she died pre-Islamic Prophetic Character Jesus / Christology Logical Inconsistency Moderate Abu Dawud #3234
"I asked my Lord for permission to beg forgiveness for my mother, but He did not grant it."

What the hadith says

Muhammad's mother Amina died before Islam. The tradition records Muhammad visiting her grave, weeping, and being denied by Allah the right to pray for her forgiveness. The implication is that she is in hell.

Why this is a problem

Amina died before Islam existed — she had no opportunity to accept it. Condemning a person for failing to embrace a message they never heard contradicts Q 35:18 (no soul bears another's burden) and the broader Islamic principle that accountability requires the message having been received. A mercy that does not extend to the prophet's own mother who predated the religion she was supposed to join is a mercy with an arbitrary boundary.

Why it fails

The rescue traditions are apologetic constructions added later; the hadith itself records a refusal. If the tradition required a special resurrection for Amina to avoid the obvious conclusion, the obvious conclusion was the tradition's original implication. A theology that requires ad hoc miracles for the prophet's mother to avoid damnation has exposed how harsh its underlying soteriological structure is.

The believer eats with one intestine; the disbeliever eats with seven Treatment of Disbelievers Strange / Obscure Cosmology Moderate Ibn Majah #2992
"The believer eats with one intestine, and the disbeliever eats with seven intestines."

What the hadith says

Ibn Majah preserves this claim in two parallel chains (parallel to Bukhari and Muslim), asserting that believers and disbelievers have anatomically different digestive systems. Classical commentators split between a literal reading (real anatomical difference) and a metaphorical one (believers eat moderately, disbelievers gluttonously).

Why this is a problem

The literal reading is anatomically false — all humans have the same intestinal architecture regardless of religious belief. The metaphorical reading reduces religious difference to a stereotype about appetite: disbelievers are gluttons, believers are moderate. Neither is defensible. The Bukhari conversion variant (#5393) records that a man drank milk from seven sheep before becoming Muslim and from one afterward — a narrative that physicalizes religious conversion as digestive transformation, which cannot be cleanly metaphorized.

Why it fails

The "metaphor is universal" claim is descriptively false: classical Sunni commentary (al-Nawawi, Ibn Hajar) records both literal and metaphorical readings. The Bukhari pre-/post-conversion eating variant cannot be cleanly metaphorized. The "modern dietetics" link is post-hoc apologetics. The metaphor reading survives because the literal one is biologically impossible — it is a face-saving translation, not the hadith's content.

Married adulterer: 100 lashes then stoning to death Women Treatment of Disbelievers Moderate Ibn Majah #2550
"The married adulterer: a hundred lashes and stoning to death."

What the hadith says

The hudud sequence for a married adulterer: 100 lashes first, then stoning to death. The two punishments are stacked.

Why this is a problem

The Quran prescribes 100 lashes for adultery (24:2); the stoning supplement derives from the claimed-removed "verse of stoning" (ayat al-rajm) whose text does not appear in the current Quran. Islam's most severe criminal penalty for consensual adult behavior rests on an absent Quranic verse. Iran, Saudi Arabia, parts of Sudan and Nigeria continue performing judicial stonings in recent decades — the "effectively inapplicable" defense fails where it is applied.

Why it fails

Naskh al-tilawa duna al-hukm concedes the core problem: the Quran's current text lacks the stoning rule, undermining its self-description as complete and preserved (15:9). The "practically inapplicable" claim does not hold in jurisdictions where judicial stonings occur. A penal code stacking flogging and execution for consensual adult sex has a design that its procedural safeguards have not eliminated.

Hand amputation at the quarter-dinar theft threshold — Ibn Majah Treatment of Disbelievers Moderate Ibn Majah #2584
"The hand is not cut off except for a quarter-dinar or more."

What the hadith says

Theft above the quarter-dinar minimum triggers hand amputation. Ibn Majah's version parallels Abu Dawud and is cross-attested in Bukhari and Muslim.

Why this is a problem

Theft is remediable — restitution can repair the harm. Amputation is permanent. The low threshold catches subsistence theft: the value of a modest purchase can trigger the loss of a hand. Saudi Arabia performed public amputations as recently as 2017. Cross-collection sahih attestation means the rule cannot be dismissed as weak material; it is canonical, operational, and disproportionate by any modern proportionality standard.

Why it fails

The procedural safeguards are juristic constructions layered over an unconditional Quranic text (Q 5:38). Where the rule is applied — Saudi Arabia, Iran, parts of Nigeria and Sudan — the "necessity" exception is not rigorously investigated. A permanent disability as the penalty for a recoverable offense is disproportionate regardless of how it is framed.
